testSuite.name: Agentic Chunking Library - Level 5 Test Cases
testSuite.description: 36 test cases covering extraction quality, grouping quality, edge cases, and full pipeline integration. Input chunks simulate Level 4 SemanticChunkingLibrary output using Cumulative pattern, windowSize=3, thresholdPct=65. Each case has explicit pass criteria.
categories: Real Pipeline Integration - 6 cases (TC-031 to TC-036)

testCases.name: Extraction Baseline - Clean Single Sentence
testCases.description: A single clean sentence with no compound structure and no pronouns. Tests that the extraction prompt passes through a well-formed proposition without modification.

chunks.text: Kubernetes is an open-source platform for automating the deployment of containerised applications.

propositions: Kubernetes is an open-source platform for automating the deployment of containerised applications.

testCases.passCriteria: Proposition count is 1. Text is unchanged or differs only in trivial whitespace. No splitting occurred. The sentence has one subject, one verb, no coordinated clauses, and no causal constructions.

testCases.name: Extraction - Compound Sentence Joined by And
testCases.description: A compound sentence joining two independent facts with 'and'. The extraction prompt must split into two atomic propositions.

chunks.text: Lithium-ion batteries store energy through the movement of lithium ions between a cathode and an anode, and the electrode materials expand and contract during each charge cycle.

propositions: Lithium-ion batteries store energy through the movement of lithium ions between a cathode and an anode.
The electrode materials in lithium-ion batteries expand and contract during each charge cycle.

testCases.passCriteria: Proposition count is 2. Each proposition is self-contained and readable in isolation. No pronouns remain unresolved. No causal inference produces a third proposition.

testCases.name: Extraction - Compound Sentence Joined by Colon
testCases.description: A compound sentence where a colon introduces a second independent fact. The extraction prompt must recognise the colon as a proposition boundary.

chunks.text: The human pancreas serves a dual function: it produces digestive enzymes that break down proteins and fats in the small intestine, and it secretes the hormones insulin and glucagon to regulate blood sugar levels.

propositions: The human pancreas produces digestive enzymes that break down proteins and fats in the small intestine.
The human pancreas secretes the hormones insulin and glucagon to regulate blood sugar levels.

testCases.passCriteria: Proposition count is 2. Both pronouns 'it' are resolved to 'the human pancreas'. Colon correctly treated as a proposition boundary.

testCases.name: Extraction - Pronoun It Referring to Named Subject
testCases.description: A sentence where 'it' refers to a named subject introduced earlier in the same sentence. The extraction prompt must replace the pronoun with the named referent.

chunks.text: The human liver is the largest internal organ, and it performs over five hundred distinct biochemical functions including detoxification, protein synthesis, and glycogen storage.

propositions: The human liver is the largest internal organ.
The human liver performs over five hundred distinct biochemical functions including detoxification, protein synthesis, and glycogen storage.

testCases.passCriteria: Proposition count is 2. Pronoun 'it' is replaced with 'the human liver' in the second proposition. No pronoun remains in any output proposition.

testCases.name: Extraction - Pronoun He Referring to Named Person
testCases.description: A sentence where 'he' refers to a named historical figure. The extraction prompt must replace the pronoun with the person's full name.

chunks.text: Leonardo da Vinci was both a painter and an engineer, and he filled private notebooks with designs for flying machines, hydraulic systems, and anatomical studies that were not published in his lifetime.

propositions: Leonardo da Vinci was both a painter and an engineer.
Leonardo da Vinci filled private notebooks with designs for flying machines, hydraulic systems, and anatomical studies that were not published in his lifetime.

testCases.passCriteria: Proposition count is 2. Pronouns 'he' and 'his' are both replaced with 'Leonardo da Vinci'. No pronoun remains in any output proposition.

testCases.name: Extraction - Pronoun They Referring to Plural Named Subject
testCases.description: A sentence where 'they' refers to a plural named subject introduced earlier. The extraction prompt must resolve the plural pronoun correctly.

chunks.text: Genome-wide association studies scan the DNA of thousands of individuals to identify disease-linked variants, and they have identified over ten thousand such associations since the first study was published in 2005.

propositions: Genome-wide association studies scan the DNA of thousands of individuals to identify disease-linked variants.
Genome-wide association studies have identified over ten thousand disease-linked associations since the first study was published in 2005.

testCases.passCriteria: Proposition count is 2. Pronoun 'they' is replaced with 'Genome-wide association studies'. Plural agreement is maintained.

testCases.name: Extraction - Compound Sentence with Embedded Pronoun
testCases.description: A compound sentence that requires both splitting and pronoun resolution in a single pass. Tests that both operations are applied correctly together.

chunks.text: CRISPR-Cas9 is a gene editing tool derived from a bacterial immune defence mechanism, and it uses a guide RNA to direct the Cas9 protein to a precise location in the genome to make a targeted cut.

propositions: CRISPR-Cas9 is a gene editing tool derived from a bacterial immune defence mechanism.
CRISPR-Cas9 uses a guide RNA to direct the Cas9 protein to a precise location in the genome to make a targeted cut.

testCases.passCriteria: Proposition count is 2. Both instances of 'it' are replaced with 'CRISPR-Cas9'. Sentence splits cleanly at the conjunction. The infinitive phrase 'to make a targeted cut' stays attached to the second proposition and is not split out as a third.

testCases.name: Extraction - Three Facts in One Sentence
testCases.description: A sentence containing three distinct facts separated by commas. The third clause references 'age-related mitochondrial decline' explicitly so the model does not need to synthesise a referent. Tests that the extraction prompt produces three atomic propositions.

chunks.text: Mitochondria generate adenosine triphosphate through oxidative phosphorylation, the efficiency of mitochondrial energy production declines with age as mitochondrial DNA accumulates mutations, and antioxidants have been proposed as a mechanism to slow age-related mitochondrial decline.

propositions: Mitochondria generate adenosine triphosphate through oxidative phosphorylation.
The efficiency of mitochondrial energy production declines with age as mitochondrial DNA accumulates mutations.
Antioxidants have been proposed as a mechanism to slow age-related mitochondrial decline.

testCases.passCriteria: Proposition count is 3. Each proposition is self-contained. The referent of 'this decline' is made explicit in the source sentence as 'age-related mitochondrial decline' so the model does not need to infer it. Causal 'as' in clause 2 is acceptable because it describes a mechanism not an invention.

testCases.name: Extraction - Relative Clause Containing Second Fact
testCases.description: A sentence where a relative clause adds a second distinct fact about the subject. The extraction prompt must recognise the relative clause as a separable proposition.

chunks.text: Polymerase chain reaction is a laboratory technique that amplifies a specific segment of DNA by cycling the sample through a series of heating and cooling steps, and it was invented by Kary Mullis in 1983 for which he received the Nobel Prize in Chemistry.

propositions: Polymerase chain reaction is a laboratory technique that amplifies a specific segment of DNA by cycling the sample through a series of heating and cooling steps.
Polymerase chain reaction was invented by Kary Mullis in 1983.
Kary Mullis received the Nobel Prize in Chemistry for inventing polymerase chain reaction.

testCases.passCriteria: Proposition count is 3. Pronoun 'it' is resolved to 'polymerase chain reaction'. The Nobel Prize fact is separated as a distinct proposition about Kary Mullis.

testCases.name: Extraction - Technical Acronym Referenced as Pronoun
testCases.description: A compound sentence where 'it' refers to a technical term introduced earlier. The parenthetical abbreviation has been removed from the source to prevent the model treating it as a separable fact. Tests pronoun resolution to a full technical term.

chunks.text: Infrastructure as code is a practice in which cloud resources are defined in machine-readable configuration files, and it allows engineering teams to version-control, review, and audit infrastructure changes using standard software development workflows.

propositions: Infrastructure as code is a practice in which cloud resources are defined in machine-readable configuration files.
Infrastructure as code allows engineering teams to version-control, review, and audit infrastructure changes using standard software development workflows.

testCases.passCriteria: Proposition count is 2. Pronoun 'it' is resolved to 'Infrastructure as code'. No third proposition is created from the parenthetical abbreviation because the parenthetical has been removed from the source. The acronym IaC does not appear in the output.

testCases.name: Grouping - Two Same-Domain Chunks Consolidated
testCases.description: Two chunks both covering Battery Chemistry that Level 4 split due to a local distance spike. Level 5 must consolidate them into one thematic chunk.

chunks.text: Solid-state batteries replace the liquid electrolyte found in conventional lithium-ion cells with a ceramic or polymer material, which eliminates the fire risk associated with electrolyte leakage.

The energy density of a battery cell is measured in watt-hours per kilogram, and current lithium-ion technology achieves between 150 and 300 watt-hours per kilogram depending on the cathode chemistry used.

propositions: Solid-state batteries replace the liquid electrolyte in conventional lithium-ion cells with a ceramic or polymer material.
The replacement electrolyte in solid-state batteries eliminates the fire risk associated with electrolyte leakage.
The energy density of a battery cell is measured in watt-hours per kilogram, and current lithium-ion technology achieves between 150 and 300 watt-hours per kilogram depending on cathode chemistry.

testCases.passCriteria: Output chunk count is 1. All propositions are assigned to Battery Chemistry. The two Level 4 chunks are consolidated despite originating as separate chunks.

testCases.name: Grouping - Three Same-Domain Chunks Consolidated
testCases.description: Three chunks covering Cloud Infrastructure that Level 4 produced separately. Level 5 must consolidate all three into one thematic chunk.

chunks.text: Cloud computing infrastructure relies on distributed server farms that are geographically separated to ensure redundancy. Major providers maintain at least three availability zones per region to guarantee uptime above 99.9 percent.

Server virtualisation allows a single physical machine to run multiple isolated operating systems simultaneously, and hypervisor software manages the allocation of CPU, memory, and storage resources between each virtual machine.

Content delivery networks cache static web assets at edge nodes located close to end users, which reduces the round-trip latency for image and script delivery to a fraction of what a single-origin server would achieve.

propositions: Cloud computing infrastructure relies on distributed server farms that are geographically separated to ensure redundancy.
Major cloud providers maintain at least three availability zones per region to guarantee uptime above 99.9 percent.
Server virtualisation allows a single physical machine to run multiple isolated operating systems simultaneously.
Hypervisor software manages the allocation of CPU, memory, and storage resources between each virtual machine.
Content delivery networks cache static web assets at edge nodes located close to end users.
Content delivery networks reduce the round-trip latency for image and script delivery compared to a single-origin server.

testCases.passCriteria: Output chunk count is 1. All six propositions are assigned to Cloud Infrastructure. Three Level 4 chunks are consolidated into one.

testCases.name: Grouping - Four Same-Domain Chunks Scattered Across Document
testCases.description: Four Battery Chemistry chunks from different positions in the interleaved test document. Tests that Level 5 can reassemble a domain that Level 4 scattered due to interleaving.

chunks.text: Lithium-ion batteries store energy through the movement of lithium ions between a cathode and an anode. This electrochemical process degrades over time as the electrode materials expand and contract during charge cycles.
Solid-state batteries replace the liquid electrolyte found in conventional lithium-ion cells with a ceramic or polymer material, which eliminates the fire risk associated with electrolyte leakage.

The cathode material in a lithium-ion battery is typically a lithium metal oxide such as lithium cobalt oxide or lithium iron phosphate, and its crystal structure determines the cell's energy density and thermal stability. Nickel manganese cobalt cathodes offer a higher energy density than lithium iron phosphate cells but are more sensitive to thermal runaway if overcharged or physically punctured.

The energy density of a battery cell is measured in watt-hours per kilogram, and current lithium-ion technology achieves between 150 and 300 watt-hours per kilogram depending on the cathode chemistry used.

propositions: Lithium-ion batteries store energy through the movement of lithium ions between a cathode and an anode.
The electrochemical process in lithium-ion batteries degrades over time as electrode materials expand and contract during charge cycles.
Solid-state batteries replace the liquid electrolyte in conventional lithium-ion cells with a ceramic or polymer material.
The ceramic or polymer electrolyte in solid-state batteries eliminates the fire risk associated with electrolyte leakage.
The cathode material in a lithium-ion battery is typically a lithium metal oxide such as lithium cobalt oxide or lithium iron phosphate.
The crystal structure of the cathode material determines the cell's energy density and thermal stability.
Nickel manganese cobalt cathodes offer a higher energy density than lithium iron phosphate cells.
Nickel manganese cobalt cathodes are more sensitive to thermal runaway if overcharged or physically punctured.

testCases.passCriteria: Output chunk count is 1. All eight propositions are assigned to Battery Chemistry. Four scattered Level 4 chunks are consolidated. Pronoun 'its' in chunk TC013-0003 is resolved to 'the cathode material's'.

testCases.name: Grouping - Mixed Domain Chunk Must Separate
testCases.description: A single Level 4 chunk containing both Human Metabolism and Genomics content that Level 4 did not split. Level 5 must separate propositions by domain before grouping.

chunks.text: The mitochondria within each human cell generate adenosine triphosphate through a process called oxidative phosphorylation, and the efficiency of this process declines with age as mitochondrial DNA accumulates mutations. The human small intestine is approximately six metres long and absorbs the majority of nutrients from digested food through finger-like projections called villi, which dramatically increase the absorptive surface area. Polymerase chain reaction is a laboratory technique that amplifies a specific segment of DNA by cycling the sample through a series of heating and cooling steps, and it was invented by Kary Mullis in 1983. DNA methylation is an epigenetic modification in which a methyl group is added to a cytosine base in the genome, and it plays a role in regulating gene expression without altering the underlying DNA sequence itself.

propositions: Mitochondria within each human cell generate adenosine triphosphate through oxidative phosphorylation.
The efficiency of oxidative phosphorylation declines with age as mitochondrial DNA accumulates mutations.
The human small intestine is approximately six metres long.
The human small intestine absorbs the majority of nutrients from digested food through finger-like projections called villi that dramatically increase the absorptive surface area.
Polymerase chain reaction is a laboratory technique that amplifies a specific segment of DNA by cycling the sample through heating and cooling steps.
Polymerase chain reaction was invented by Kary Mullis in 1983.
DNA methylation is an epigenetic modification in which a methyl group is added to a cytosine base in the genome.
DNA methylation plays a role in regulating gene expression without altering the underlying DNA sequence.

testCases.passCriteria: Output chunk count is 2. Propositions 1 through 4 are assigned to Human Metabolism. Propositions 5 through 8 are assigned to Genomics. A single Level 4 chunk is correctly separated into two thematic chunks.

testCases.name: Grouping - Clean Single-Domain Chunks Pass Through Unchanged
testCases.description: Three chunks each clearly from a different domain with no over-splitting and no mixing. Level 5 should extract propositions and assign one chunk per domain without merging or splitting.

chunks.text: Filippo Brunelleschi designed the dome of Florence Cathedral without the use of temporary wooden centring, relying instead on a herringbone brick pattern that allowed each course of bricks to be self-supporting during construction.

Urban planners use a metric called the floor area ratio to regulate building density, and cities that adopt high floor area ratios near transit stations typically see reduced car dependency within a decade.
Genome-wide association studies scan the DNA of thousands of individuals to identify single nucleotide polymorphisms that correlate with a particular disease or trait.

propositions: Filippo Brunelleschi designed the dome of Florence Cathedral without the use of temporary wooden centring.
Brunelleschi used a herringbone brick pattern that allowed each course of bricks to be self-supporting during construction.
Urban planners use a metric called the floor area ratio to regulate building density.
Cities that adopt high floor area ratios near transit stations typically see reduced car dependency within a decade.

testCases.passCriteria: Output chunk count is 3. No consolidation occurs. No splitting occurs. Each input chunk maps to exactly one output chunk with the correct thematic label.

testCases.name: Grouping - Adjacent Chunks from Different Domains Must Not Bleed
testCases.description: Two chunks from different domains that are positionally adjacent. Urban Planning content sits immediately after Italian Renaissance content. The grouper must keep them separate despite proximity.

chunks.text: Michelangelo painted the Sistine Chapel ceiling between 1508 and 1512 under the patronage of Pope Julius II, and the work depicts scenes from the Book of Genesis including the creation of Adam.

Mixed-use zoning permits residential, retail, and office uses to coexist within the same block or building, and it is associated with higher pedestrian activity and lower vehicle kilometres travelled compared to single-use suburban zoning.

propositions: Michelangelo painted the Sistine Chapel ceiling between 1508 and 1512 under the patronage of Pope Julius II.
The Sistine Chapel ceiling depicts scenes from the Book of Genesis including the creation of Adam.

Mixed-use zoning permits residential, retail, and office uses to coexist within the same block or building and is associated with higher pedestrian activity and lower vehicle kilometres travelled compared to single-use suburban zoning.

testCases.passCriteria: Output chunk count is 2. No Renaissance proposition appears in the Urban Planning chunk and vice versa. Positional adjacency does not cause thematic bleeding.

testCases.name: Grouping - Single Proposition Domain
testCases.description: One domain is represented by a single atomic proposition only. Level 5 must still assign it its own thematic chunk rather than merging it with a loosely related domain.

chunks.text: The sixteen-hour city concept proposes that urban districts should be activated for at least sixteen hours per day through a mix of uses and programming rather than emptying after business hours.

Raphael completed the School of Athens fresco in the Apostolic Palace of the Vatican between 1509 and 1511, and it depicts an imagined gathering of ancient Greek philosophers including Plato and Aristotle in a grand classical architectural setting.

propositions: The sixteen-hour city concept proposes that urban districts should be activated for at least sixteen hours per day through a mix of uses and programming.
Raphael completed the School of Athens fresco in the Apostolic Palace of the Vatican between 1509 and 1511.
The School of Athens fresco depicts an imagined gathering of ancient Greek philosophers including Plato and Aristotle in a grand classical architectural setting.

testCases.passCriteria: Output chunk count is 2. The single Urban Planning proposition receives its own chunk. It is not merged into Italian Renaissance despite both involving historical or cultural content.

testCases.name: Grouping - Overlapping Vocabulary Across Domains
testCases.description: Cloud Infrastructure and Genomics both use terms like 'cluster', 'sequence', and 'node' in different contexts. The grouper must separate them by domain meaning not by surface vocabulary.

chunks.text: Kubernetes manages containerised applications across a cluster of machines by scheduling workloads onto nodes based on available CPU and memory resources.

DNA sequencing determines the precise order of nucleotides within a DNA molecule, and next-generation sequencing platforms can process billions of short sequence reads in parallel across a single run.

propositions: Kubernetes manages containerised applications across a cluster of machines by scheduling workloads onto nodes based on available CPU and memory resources.
DNA sequencing determines the precise order of nucleotides within a DNA molecule.
Next-generation sequencing platforms can process billions of short sequence reads in parallel across a single run.

testCases.passCriteria: Output chunk count is 2. 'Cluster' and 'node' in the Cloud Infrastructure context are correctly separated from 'sequence' in the Genomics context. Vocabulary overlap does not cause incorrect merging.

testCases.name: Grouping - All Sentences Are Definitions
testCases.description: All chunks contain definitional sentences of the form 'X is Y'. The grouper must group by subject domain not by the definitional sentence structure which is shared across all chunks.

chunks.text: A hypervisor is software that creates and manages virtual machines by abstracting the underlying hardware resources. A container is a lightweight, portable unit of software that packages an application and its dependencies into a single executable environment.

An allele is one of two or more versions of a gene that can exist at a given chromosomal location. A phenotype is the observable physical or biochemical characteristics of an organism resulting from the interaction of its genotype and environment.

propositions: A hypervisor is software that creates and manages virtual machines by abstracting the underlying hardware resources.
A container is a lightweight, portable unit of software that packages an application and its dependencies into a single executable environment.
An allele is one of two or more versions of a gene that can exist at a given chromosomal location.
A phenotype is the observable physical or biochemical characteristics of an organism resulting from the interaction of its genotype and environment.

testCases.passCriteria: Output chunk count is 2. Definitional sentence structure does not cause incorrect merging. Propositions 1 and 2 are assigned to Cloud Infrastructure. Propositions 3 and 4 are assigned to Genomics.

testCases.name: Grouping - Proposition Plausibly Belonging to Two Domains
testCases.description: A proposition about computational biology that plausibly belongs to either Cloud Infrastructure or Genomics. Tests that the grouper makes a deterministic assignment and does not duplicate the proposition across two chunks.

chunks.text: Bioinformatics pipelines process raw genome sequencing data using distributed computing clusters, applying quality filters, alignment algorithms, and variant callers across hundreds of parallel compute nodes.
Kubernetes is a container orchestration platform that automates the deployment and scaling of containerised applications across a cluster of machines.

Whole genome sequencing produces approximately 200 gigabytes of raw data per human sample, and storing and processing this volume at scale requires purpose-built cloud storage and compute infrastructure.

propositions: Bioinformatics pipelines process raw genome sequencing data using distributed computing clusters, applying quality filters, alignment algorithms, and variant callers across parallel compute nodes.
Kubernetes is a container orchestration platform that automates the deployment and scaling of containerised applications across a cluster of machines.
Whole genome sequencing produces approximately 200 gigabytes of raw data per human sample.
Storing and processing whole genome sequencing data at scale requires purpose-built cloud storage and compute infrastructure.

expectedGroupingOutput.ambiguousPropositionNote: Proposition 1 (bioinformatics pipelines) may be assigned to either Genomics or Cloud Infrastructure. Either assignment is acceptable. The key pass criterion is that it appears in exactly one chunk, not both.

testCases.passCriteria: Output chunk count is 2. The bioinformatics proposition appears in exactly one chunk. No proposition is duplicated across two chunks.

testCases.name: Edge Case - Single Chunk Single Sentence
testCases.description: The minimum viable input: one chunk containing one sentence. Tests that the pipeline completes without error and returns one proposition in one chunk.

chunks.text: The floor area ratio is a measure of the total floor area of a building relative to the size of the land parcel on which it stands.

propositions: The floor area ratio is a measure of the total floor area of a building relative to the size of the land parcel on which it stands.

testCases.passCriteria: Pipeline completes without error. Proposition count is 1. Chunk count is 1. No null reference errors or empty responses.

testCases.name: Edge Case - Single Chunk Ten Sentences Same Domain
testCases.description: One chunk containing ten sentences all from the same domain. No consolidation is needed. Tests that the extraction phase handles a larger single-chunk input correctly and that the grouper does not over-split.

chunks.text: Lithium-ion batteries power most consumer electronics including smartphones, laptops, and electric vehicles. The anode in a lithium-ion cell is typically made from graphite which intercalates lithium ions during charging. The cathode is typically a lithium metal oxide whose composition determines the cell's voltage and capacity. The electrolyte is a lithium salt dissolved in an organic solvent that conducts ions between the electrodes. A separator membrane prevents direct contact between the anode and cathode while allowing ion flow. During charging, lithium ions move from the cathode through the electrolyte to the anode. During discharging, lithium ions return from the anode to the cathode, releasing energy. The charge-discharge cycle causes mechanical stress on the electrode materials that limits the battery's lifespan. Thermal management systems regulate battery temperature to prevent degradation and thermal runaway. Recycling programmes recover lithium, cobalt, and nickel from spent cells to reduce dependence on primary mining.

propositions: Lithium-ion batteries power most consumer electronics including smartphones, laptops, and electric vehicles.
The anode in a lithium-ion cell is typically made from graphite which intercalates lithium ions during charging.
The cathode in a lithium-ion cell is typically a lithium metal oxide whose composition determines the cell's voltage and capacity.
The electrolyte in a lithium-ion cell is a lithium salt dissolved in an organic solvent that conducts ions between the electrodes.
A separator membrane prevents direct contact between the anode and cathode while allowing ion flow.
During charging, lithium ions move from the cathode through the electrolyte to the anode.
During discharging, lithium ions return from the anode to the cathode, releasing energy.
The charge-discharge cycle causes mechanical stress on the electrode materials that limits the battery's lifespan.
Thermal management systems regulate battery temperature to prevent degradation and thermal runaway.
Recycling programmes recover lithium, cobalt, and nickel from spent cells to reduce dependence on primary mining.

testCases.passCriteria: Proposition count is 10. Output chunk count is 1. The grouper does not split the single-domain content into multiple chunks.

testCases.name: Edge Case - All Chunks Same Domain
testCases.description: All input chunks belong to one domain. Level 5 must consolidate everything into a single output chunk.

chunks.text: A transit-oriented development clusters residential and commercial buildings within a ten-minute walk of a rail or bus rapid transit station.
Mixed-use zoning permits residential, retail, and office uses to coexist within the same block, and it is associated with higher pedestrian activity and lower vehicle kilometres travelled.
The sixteen-hour city concept proposes that urban districts should be activated for at least sixteen hours per day through a mix of uses and programming rather than emptying after business hours.

propositions: A transit-oriented development clusters residential and commercial buildings within a ten-minute walk of a rail or bus rapid transit station.
Mixed-use zoning permits residential, retail, and office uses to coexist within the same block.
Mixed-use zoning is associated with higher pedestrian activity and lower vehicle kilometres travelled.
The sixteen-hour city concept proposes that urban districts should be activated for at least sixteen hours per day through a mix of uses and programming rather than emptying after business hours.

testCases.passCriteria: Output chunk count is 1. All propositions are assigned to Urban Planning. Three Level 4 chunks consolidate to one output chunk.

testCases.name: Edge Case - Every Chunk a Different Domain
testCases.description: Six chunks each from a different domain with no over-splitting. No consolidation is needed. Tests that Level 5 correctly assigns one chunk per domain without attempting to merge.

chunks.text: Kubernetes is a container orchestration platform that automates the deployment and scaling of containerised applications.
The human liver converts ammonia to urea for excretion by the kidneys.
Michelangelo painted the Sistine Chapel ceiling between 1508 and 1512 under the patronage of Pope Julius II.
Nickel manganese cobalt cathodes offer a higher energy density than lithium iron phosphate cells.
CRISPR-Cas9 uses a guide RNA to direct the Cas9 protein to a precise location in the genome where it makes a targeted cut.
The floor area ratio is used by urban planners to regulate building density relative to land parcel size.

propositions: Kubernetes is a container orchestration platform that automates the deployment and scaling of containerised applications.
The human liver converts ammonia to urea for excretion by the kidneys.
Michelangelo painted the Sistine Chapel ceiling between 1508 and 1512 under the patronage of Pope Julius II.
Nickel manganese cobalt cathodes offer a higher energy density than lithium iron phosphate cells.
CRISPR-Cas9 uses a guide RNA to direct the Cas9 protein to a precise location in the genome where it makes a targeted cut.
The floor area ratio is used by urban planners to regulate building density relative to land parcel size.

testCases.passCriteria: Output chunk count is 6. No consolidation occurs. Each input chunk maps to exactly one output chunk with the correct thematic label. CRISPR-Cas9 pronoun 'it' is resolved.

testCases.name: Edge Case - Empty Text in One Chunk
testCases.description: One chunk contains an empty string. The pipeline must handle this gracefully without throwing an exception and must process the remaining valid chunks correctly.

chunks.text: The human liver performs over five hundred distinct biochemical functions.
Genome-wide association studies identify single nucleotide polymorphisms that correlate with a particular disease or trait.

propositions: The human liver performs over five hundred distinct biochemical functions.
Genome-wide association studies identify single nucleotide polymorphisms that correlate with a particular disease or trait.

testCases.passCriteria: Pipeline completes without error. Empty chunk is skipped silently. Valid chunks are processed correctly. Output chunk count is 2.

testCases.name: Edge Case - Very Long Chunk Approaching Token Limit
testCases.description: A single chunk with content approaching the token envelope for the extraction prompt. Tests that PreChunkForExtraction correctly batches this into safe prompt sizes.

chunks.text: Lithium-ion batteries power most consumer electronics and electric vehicles by storing energy through ion movement between electrodes. The anode is typically graphite which intercalates lithium ions during charging. The cathode is a lithium metal oxide whose crystal structure determines energy density. The electrolyte conducts ions between the electrodes and must remain chemically stable across the operating voltage range. A polymer separator prevents short circuits while allowing ion transport. During charging the external power source forces lithium ions from cathode to anode. During discharging the ions return spontaneously releasing stored energy as electricity. Cycle life degrades as repeated expansion and contraction damages the electrode crystal structure. Calendar ageing occurs even without cycling as the electrolyte slowly decomposes. Solid electrolyte interphase formation on the anode consumes lithium and reduces capacity on early cycles. Thermal runaway occurs when internal heat generation exceeds the battery's ability to dissipate heat. Overcharge protection circuits prevent voltage from exceeding safe limits. Battery management systems monitor individual cell voltages, temperatures, and state of charge. Nickel manganese cobalt chemistry offers higher energy density at the cost of thermal sensitivity. Lithium iron phosphate chemistry offers lower energy density with superior thermal stability and cycle life. Silicon anodes can store ten times more lithium than graphite but suffer from severe volume expansion. Solid-state batteries replace liquid electrolyte with ceramic or polymer material eliminating leakage risk. Energy density is measured in watt-hours per kilogram with current technology achieving 150 to 300. Power density is measured in watts per kilogram and determines peak discharge capability. Second-life applications repurpose retired electric vehicle batteries for stationary grid storage.

expectedExtractionOutput.note: Each sentence produces one proposition. No compound sentences requiring splitting are present. All twenty sentences are already atomic.

testCases.passCriteria: Pipeline completes without timeout. PreChunkForExtraction handles the token envelope correctly. All 20 propositions are extracted. Output chunk count is 1.

testCases.name: Edge Case - Numbered List Embedded in Prose
testCases.description: A chunk containing a numbered list embedded within prose. The extraction prompt must produce propositions from the list items as well as from the surrounding prose sentences.

chunks.text: Urban planners use several tools to reduce car dependency in city centres. These include: 1) increasing the floor area ratio near transit stations, 2) requiring mixed-use ground floor activation on arterial streets, 3) reducing minimum parking requirements for new developments, and 4) implementing road pricing to internalise the cost of private vehicle use.

propositions: Urban planners use several tools to reduce car dependency in city centres.
Increasing the floor area ratio near transit stations reduces car dependency.
Requiring mixed-use ground floor activation on arterial streets reduces car dependency.
Reducing minimum parking requirements for new developments reduces car dependency.
Implementing road pricing internalises the cost of private vehicle use and reduces car dependency.

testCases.passCriteria: Proposition count is 5. Each numbered list item becomes a standalone proposition with an explicit subject. The list numbering is dropped from the output.

testCases.name: Edge Case - Chunk Containing a Quoted Statement
testCases.description: A chunk where a key fact is presented as a direct quote. The extraction prompt must attribute the quote correctly in the proposition rather than treating it as an anonymous statement.

chunks.text: Filippo Brunelleschi is reported to have said that the dome could be built without centring if the bricklayers followed the herringbone pattern precisely. Giorgio Vasari recorded this account in his Lives of the Artists published in 1550.

propositions: Filippo Brunelleschi claimed that the dome of Florence Cathedral could be built without centring if bricklayers followed the herringbone pattern precisely.
Giorgio Vasari recorded Brunelleschi's account of the dome construction in his Lives of the Artists, published in 1550.

testCases.passCriteria: Proposition count is 2. Attribution to Brunelleschi is preserved in proposition 1. Attribution to Vasari is preserved in proposition 2. No fact is presented as unattributed.

testCases.name: Edge Case - Topic Shift Mid-Paragraph That Level 4 Missed
testCases.description: A chunk where the topic shifts mid-paragraph but Level 4 did not split it because the cosine distance at that boundary was below threshold. Level 5 must detect the shift during proposition extraction and assign propositions to the correct domains during grouping.

chunks.text: Content delivery networks reduce latency by caching assets at edge nodes close to end users. Kubernetes automates the deployment of containerised workloads across those same distributed infrastructure nodes. DNA methylation adds a methyl group to cytosine bases in the genome to regulate gene expression. Epigenetic changes like methylation can be inherited across cell divisions without altering the DNA sequence itself.

propositions: Content delivery networks reduce latency by caching assets at edge nodes close to end users.
Kubernetes automates the deployment of containerised workloads across distributed infrastructure nodes.
DNA methylation adds a methyl group to cytosine bases in the genome to regulate gene expression.
Epigenetic changes like methylation can be inherited across cell divisions without altering the DNA sequence itself.

testCases.passCriteria: Output chunk count is 2. Propositions 1 and 2 are assigned to Cloud Infrastructure. Propositions 3 and 4 are assigned to Genomics. A topic shift that Level 4 missed is correctly resolved by Level 5 at the grouping stage.

testCases.name: Edge Case - Duplicate Propositions Across Two Chunks
testCases.description: The same factual proposition appears in two separate chunks due to overlap in the source document. Level 5 must not duplicate it in the output. Each proposition should appear in exactly one thematic chunk.

chunks.text: Lithium-ion batteries store energy through the movement of lithium ions between electrodes. The energy density of a battery cell is measured in watt-hours per kilogram.
The energy density of a battery cell is measured in watt-hours per kilogram. Current lithium-ion technology achieves between 150 and 300 watt-hours per kilogram depending on cathode chemistry.

propositions: Lithium-ion batteries store energy through the movement of lithium ions between electrodes.
The energy density of a battery cell is measured in watt-hours per kilogram.
Current lithium-ion technology achieves between 150 and 300 watt-hours per kilogram depending on cathode chemistry.
expectedExtractionOutput.note: The duplicate energy density sentence from TC030-0002 is deduplicated during extraction. Only 3 unique propositions are produced from 4 source sentences.

testCases.passCriteria: Proposition count is 3 not 4. The energy density proposition appears exactly once. Output chunk count is 1.

testCases.name: Integration - Full Six-Domain Document End to End
testCases.description: Full end-to-end run using the AgenticChunkingTestInput.json primary test file. 12 Level 4 chunks covering six interleaved domains are processed through the complete Level 5 pipeline. This is the primary integration test.

expectedExtractionOutput.note: Exact count depends on how the model handles the compound sentences and pronoun resolution targets documented in AgenticChunkingTestInput.json annotations.

testCases.passCriteria: Extraction phase: all five compound sentences are split, all five pronoun targets are resolved. Grouping phase: Battery Chemistry propositions from all four source chunks land in one output chunk. Mixed-domain chunk DOC-0012 is separated correctly. Output chunk count is between 5 and 8.

testCases.name: Integration - Single Domain Document
testCases.description: A document where all content belongs to one domain, processed through the full pipeline. All Level 4 chunks must consolidate to one output chunk.

input.sourceDescription: A five-paragraph plain text document covering only Battery Chemistry. Run through SplitBySentence then GroupBySemantic Cumulative windowSize=3 thresholdPct=65 to generate Level 4 input.

testCases.passCriteria: All Level 4 chunks consolidate to one output chunk. No spurious second domain is created. Output chunk count is 1.

testCases.name: Integration - Full Five-Level Stack
testCases.description: A structured Markdown document processed through the full five-level pipeline: Level 3 Markdown splitting, Level 4 Cumulative semantic grouping, Level 5 agentic chunking. Tests that the data contract holds across all three levels.

input.sourceDescription: A structured Markdown document with headings covering three domains: Cloud Infrastructure, Battery Chemistry, and Genomics. Run through ChunkMarkdown (Level 3), then GroupBySemantic Cumulative (Level 4), then Level 5.

testCases.passCriteria: Data contract holds across all three levels. Level 3 plain text output feeds Level 4 correctly. Level 4 SemanticChunkResult list feeds Level 5 correctly. Final output produces 3 thematic chunks. No pipeline errors at any level boundary.

testCases.name: Integration - Short Document Two Domains
testCases.description: A minimal four-sentence document with two domains run through the complete pipeline. Tests the pipeline with the smallest realistic multi-domain input.

input.sourceDescription: Four sentences: two about Human Metabolism, two about Urban Planning. Run through SplitBySentence then GroupBySemantic Cumulative windowSize=3 thresholdPct=65.

testCases.passCriteria: Output chunk count is 2. Each domain receives exactly one chunk. Pipeline completes without error on minimal input.

testCases.name: Integration - Enterprise Policy Document Excerpt
testCases.description: A real-world excerpt from an enterprise data privacy policy run through the full pipeline. Tests Level 5 on content that is not purpose-built for chunking, where domain boundaries may be less clear-cut than in the six-domain test document.

input.sourceDescription: A 500 to 800 word excerpt from a data privacy policy covering sections on data collection, data retention, user rights, and third-party sharing. Run through SplitBySentence then GroupBySemantic Cumulative windowSize=3 thresholdPct=65.

expectedExtractionOutput.note: Policy documents use formal language with fewer compound sentences and fewer pronouns than prose. Proposition count may be close to sentence count.

expectedGroupingOutput.note: Theme names will be generated by the LLM based on content. Exact labels may vary.

testCases.passCriteria: Pipeline completes on real-world content. Output chunks correspond to recognisable policy sections. No propositions are lost. Thematic labels are descriptive and accurate. Chunk count is between 3 and 5.

testCases.name: Integration - Cross-Chunk Consolidation and Mixed-Domain Split
testCases.description: Four realistic multi-sentence Level 4 chunks (2 to 4 sentences each), unlike TC-001 through TC-030 which isolate single sentences. Two chunks (ART-0001, ART-0003) are both Battery Chemistry but were separated by an unrelated Urban Planning chunk in the source document, simulating what happens when a document interleaves topics. One chunk (ART-0004) silently mixes Human Metabolism and Genomics content because vocabulary overlap kept the Level 4 cosine distance under threshold. This is the primary demonstration case for the article: it shows Level 5 correcting chunk topology rather than simply changing chunk count.

chunks.text: Lithium-ion batteries store energy through the movement of lithium ions between a cathode and an anode. This electrochemical process is the basis for nearly all modern portable electronics and electric vehicles. The cathode material is typically a lithium metal oxide, and its specific composition determines the cell's overall energy density.

Urban planners use the floor area ratio to regulate building density relative to the size of a land parcel. Cities that pair high floor area ratios with strong public transit access tend to see reduced car dependency over time. Mixed-use zoning reinforces this effect by allowing residential and commercial activity to coexist on the same block.

Solid-state batteries replace the liquid electrolyte found in conventional lithium-ion cells with a ceramic or polymer material. This substitution removes the leakage and fire risk associated with liquid electrolytes. Energy density in current solid-state designs is measured in watt-hours per kilogram and is approaching parity with the best lithium-ion chemistries.

Mitochondria generate adenosine triphosphate through a process called oxidative phosphorylation. The efficiency of this process declines with age as mitochondrial DNA accumulates mutations. DNA methylation is a separate epigenetic mechanism in which a methyl group is added to a cytosine base in the genome. This modification regulates gene expression without altering the underlying DNA sequence.

expectedExtractionOutput.note: Each multi-sentence chunk yields multiple propositions. The cathode composition sentence in ART-0001 and the methylation sentence in ART-0004 each contain a relative clause likely to split into two propositions.

consolidationExpected.note: These two source chunks are not adjacent in the input list (ART-0002 sits between them) yet must consolidate into one output chunk. This is the primary cross-chunk consolidation test in the suite.

splitExpected.note: A single Level 4 chunk silently mixing two domains due to vocabulary overlap (DNA, genome, cellular biology). Level 5 must detect the boundary between the mitochondria sentences and the methylation sentences that Level 4 missed.

unchangedExpected.note: Already correctly grouped by Level 4. Tests that Level 5 does not over-process content that needs no correction.

testCases.passCriteria: Output chunk count is 4 (or within 3 to 5 if the grouper subdivides further). ART-0001 and ART-0003 propositions land in a single Battery Chemistry chunk despite originating from non-adjacent source chunks. ART-0004 propositions split correctly into Human Metabolism and Genomics, with no metabolism proposition in the Genomics chunk and vice versa. ART-0002 propositions remain together as Urban Planning, demonstrating no unnecessary splitting of already-correct content.
